perturbation.original_text: StrikeForce and Company are interested in disclosing to each other certain information relating to their respective business plans and proprietary technology (hereinafter "Information") for the purposes of evaluation and consultation. For the purposes of this Agreement, each party shall be in the position of "Disclosing Party" for the Information it discloses to the other party, and each party shall be in the position of "Recipient" for the Information it receives from the other party. Such Information is a commercial asset of considerable value to Disclosing Party, and Disclosing Party is willing to disclose such Information only under the terms and conditions set forth below.
perturbation.changed_text: StrikeForce and Company are interested in disclosing to each other certain information relating to their respective business plans and proprietary technology (hereinafter "Information") for the purposes of evaluation and consultation. For the purposes of this Agreement, each party shall be in the position of "Disclosing Party" for the Information it discloses to the other party, and each party shall be in the position of "Recipient" for the Information it receives from the other party. Such Information is a commercial asset of considerable value to Disclosing Party, and Disclosing Party is willing to disclose such Information only under general terms and conditions.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'terms and conditions set forth below' to 'general terms and conditions', the contract becomes ambiguous. It suggests specific terms exist elsewhere, however, as this agreement is meant to be a standalone document the reader is left wondering where these general terms are located. The contradiction lies within section 5 which refers to the agreement being effective as of the Effective Date.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be effective as of the Effective Date and may be terminated by either party upon thirty (30) day's prior written notice to the other party. In any event, this Agreement shall automatically terminate two (2) years after the Effective Date. The confidentiality and use restrictions with respect to Proprietary and Confidential Information disclosed prior to termination shall survive for a period of two (2) years after the termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'terms and conditions set forth below,' implying specific and defined terms within the agreement. The changed text alters this to 'general terms and conditions,' creating ambiguity about where these terms are located, suggesting they exist outside the document. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states 'This Agreement shall be effective as of the Effective Date,' suggesting a self-contained agreement. The change introduces an external reference, undermining the agreement's standalone nature, thereby creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined in Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Information shall include such proprietary and confidential information disclosed orally, by demonstration, or in writing at any time, and may include without limitation business plans, know-how, source code, algorithms, flow-charts, blueprints, and other information not readily available to the general public, whether or not protectable by patent, copyright or other forms of intellectual property law. The Information does not need to be identified as or marked "confidential" or "proprietary" or any similar terms.
perturbation.changed_text: The Information shall include such proprietary and confidential information disclosed orally, by demonstration, or in writing at any time, and may include without limitation business plans, know-how, source code, algorithms, flow-charts, blueprints, and other information not readily available to the general public. The Information does not need to be identified as or marked "confidential" or "proprietary" or any similar terms.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'whether or not protectable by patent, copyright or other forms of intellectual property law', the definition of Information becomes unclear. Now it's ambiguous whether information that *is* protected by IP law is covered. Section 4 mentions intellectual property rights, so there is a contradiction as to what intellectual property is covered.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed by implication or otherwise, as an obligation to enter into any further agreement relating to the Information or as grant of a license to use the Information or any intellectual property rights therein other than for evaluation and consultation purposes. Disclosing Party retains any and all proprietary and ownership rights it has in and to the Information it discloses.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'whether or not protectable by patent, copyright or other forms of intellectual property law' from the definition of 'Information'. This creates ambiguity as to whether information protected by IP law is covered under the agreement. The contradicted text states that the disclosing party retains all proprietary and ownership rights in the Information, which implies that the Information includes items that are protected by intellectual property rights. The changed text introduces a contradiction by making it unclear if such protected information is included, conflicting with the intent of the contradicted text to protect the disclosing party's rights. This ambiguity could lead to disputes over intellectual property rights and creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall hold the Information in confidence, and shall use reasonable efforts to prevent any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Information. Except as expressly provided in this Agreement, the Recipient shall not disclose or divulge the Information, in whole or in part, to any third party, including licensees or customers anywhere in the world. The Recipient may not use the Information for any purpose other than the aforesaid without the prior written consent of a duly authorized representative of Disclosing Party. The Recipient may disclose the Information only to its officers, employees and independent contractors who are necessary for the purpose of evaluating such Information, and Recipient shall be responsible for any disclosure by them in violation of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient shall hold the Information in confidence. Except as expressly provided in this Agreement, the Recipient shall not disclose or divulge the Information, in whole or in part, to any third party, including licensees or customers anywhere in the world. The Recipient may not use the Information for any purpose other than the aforesaid without the prior written consent of a duly authorized representative of Disclosing Party. The Recipient may disclose the Information only to its officers, employees and independent contractors who are necessary for the purpose of evaluating such Information, and Recipient shall be responsible for any disclosure by them in violation of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and shall use reasonable efforts to prevent any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Information', the level of care required of the Recipient becomes undefined. Section 7 states in event of a violation of the agreement the disclosing party can seek a judgement, therefore, the level of 'efforts' to hold the information in confidence can not be objectively measured since it was omitted in this section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties hereby acknowledge and agree that in the event of any violation or a threatened violation of this Agreement by the Recipient, the Disclosing Party shall be authorized and entitled to seek from any court of competent jurisdiction preliminary and permanent injunctive relief. This Agreement shall be governed by the law of the United States of America and the State of New Jersey without regard to conflicts of laws principles. Sections 3-7 shall survive any termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Recipient 'shall use reasonable efforts to prevent any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Information.' The changed text omits this clause, thereby removing the explicit requirement for 'reasonable efforts'. The contradicted text states that the Disclosing Party can seek injunctive relief in the event of a violation of the agreement. Because the 'reasonable efforts' clause was removed in the changed text, this leads to a contradiction because the degree of effort required is now undefined, making it difficult to determine if a violation occurred, and therefore affecting the Disclosing Party's ability to seek injunctive relief as stated in the contradicted text. This aligns with the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition, since essential language was removed creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed by implication or otherwise, as an obligation to enter into any further agreement relating to the Information or as grant of a license to use the Information or any intellectual property rights therein other than for evaluation and consultation purposes. Disclosing Party retains any and all proprietary and ownership rights it has in and to the Information it discloses.
perturbation.changed_text: Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed by implication or otherwise, as an obligation to enter into any further agreement relating to the Information or as grant of a license to use the Information other than for evaluation and consultation purposes. Disclosing Party retains any and all proprietary and ownership rights it has in and to the Information it discloses.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the words 'or any intellectual property rights therein', the sentence now suggests that the agreement grants a license to use the Information itself, but doesn't address intellectual property rights related to the Information. This contradicts section 6, which implies the recipient must cease all use of the information and make no further use of it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon completion of the aforesaid evaluation and in the absence of further agreement of the parties, the Recipient shall cease all use and make no further use of the Information. At Disclosing Party's request, the Recipient shall promptly return or destroy all Information disclosed by Disclosing Party and shall retain no copies.
perturbation.justification: The original text prevents construing the agreement as granting a license to use the Information 'or any intellectual property rights therein'. The changed text removes 'or any intellectual property rights therein', which now implies a license to use the information itself MAY be granted (though only for evaluation and consultation). This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which states that upon completion of evaluation and without further agreement, the Recipient must 'cease all use and make no further use of the Information', and must return or destroy all information, effectively prohibiting any continued use of the information, which includes what might now be interpreted as licensed use according to the Changed Text.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be effective as of the Effective Date and may be terminated by either party upon thirty (30) day's prior written notice to the other party. In any event, this Agreement shall automatically terminate two (2) years after the Effective Date. The confidentiality and use restrictions with respect to Proprietary and Confidential Information disclosed prior to termination shall survive for a period of two (2) years after the termination.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be effective as of the Effective Date and may be terminated by either party upon written notice to the other party. In any event, this Agreement shall automatically terminate two (2) years after the Effective Date. The confidentiality and use restrictions with respect to Proprietary and Confidential Information disclosed prior to termination shall survive for a period of two (2) years after the termination.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'thirty (30) day's prior', the clause is now ambiguous about the notice period required for termination. Section 7 mentions violations and threatened violations so if a party can terminate without a 30 days notice that is a potential breach of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties hereby acknowledge and agree that in the event of any violation or a threatened violation of this Agreement by the Recipient, the Disclosing Party shall be authorized and entitled to seek from any court of competent jurisdiction preliminary and permanent injunctive relief. This Agreement shall be governed by the law of the United States of America and the State of New Jersey without regard to conflicts of laws principles. Sections 3-7 shall survive any termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a 30-day written notice for termination. The changed text removes 'thirty (30) day's prior', allowing for immediate termination by either party. The contradicted text states that the disclosing party can seek injunctive relief for any violation or threatened violation. If a party terminates the agreement without the 30-day notice that was originally agreed upon, it could be considered a breach, which now can happen at any time because the 30 day notice has been removed, leading to the Disclosing party being able to seek injunctive relief. Therefore, the removal of the notice period creates a contradiction by allowing for potential breaches of contract and triggering the contradicted clause.

perturbation.original_text: Upon completion of the aforesaid evaluation and in the absence of further agreement of the parties, the Recipient shall cease all use and make no further use of the Information. At Disclosing Party's request, the Recipient shall promptly return or destroy all Information disclosed by Disclosing Party and shall retain no copies.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon completion of the aforesaid evaluation and in the absence of further agreement of the parties, the Recipient shall make no further use of the Information. At Disclosing Party's request, the Recipient shall promptly return or destroy all Information disclosed by Disclosing Party and shall retain no copies.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'the Recipient shall cease all use and' The recipient is no longer required to cease use of information, however they can't make further use of the information which creates a contradiction since those actions would involve further use of the information. Section 3 and 7 refers to the restriction on usage and further agreement of the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient shall hold the Information in confidence, and shall use reasonable efforts to prevent any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Information. Except as expressly provided in this Agreement, the Recipient shall not disclose or divulge the Information, in whole or in part, to any third party, including licensees or customers anywhere in the world. The Recipient may not use the Information for any purpose other than the aforesaid without the prior written consent of a duly authorized representative of Disclosing Party. The Recipient may disclose the Information only to its officers, employees and independent contractors who are necessary for the purpose of evaluating such Information, and Recipient shall be responsible for any disclosure by them in violation of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated, 'the Recipient shall cease all use and make no further use of the Information.' The changed text removes 'the Recipient shall cease all use and', leaving only 'the Recipient shall make no further use of the Information.' According to the explanation, this creates a contradiction because the Recipient is no longer required to cease use, but cannot make 'further use,' which is inherently contradictory. The contradicted text states, 'The Recipient may not use the Information for any purpose other than the aforesaid without the prior written consent...' This text now contradicts the changed text because it restricts any usage without consent, whereas the changed text implies usage is acceptable as long as it's not 'further use.' This meets the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.
